title: chord.bindings.v3
description: Wire format emitted by `chord config --show --json` and `chord config --validate --json`. Version chord.bindings.v3 (was v2 through 0.5.x, v1 through 0.3.x). v3 renames over v2: `aliases` → `action_aliases` (TOML `[aliases]` → `[action-aliases]`); warning kinds `undefined-alias` → `undefined-action-alias`, `alias-non-string` → `action-alias-non-string`; section enum `[aliases]` → `[action-aliases]`; new kind `undefined-input-alias` for `$name` references that fail to resolve. v3 also requires the `$` prefix on `[input-aliases]` references in `input = "…"` (was bare in v2.x). Emitters: chord >= 0.6.0. Breaking changes require a major bump (v4) — consumers should pin the schema and treat unknown fields as forward-compatible additions. **Consumer pinning guidance**: prefer a tagged URL (e.g. `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/akira-toriyama/chord/v0.6.0/docs/schema/chord.bindings.v3.json`) over `…/main/…` for reproducibility — `main` is a moving reference. The strongest option is to vendor this file into the consumer repo (e.g. `docs/external/chord.bindings.v3.json`), giving network-independence and explicit diff review when chord ships a new schema version. See https://github.com/akira-toriyama/chord/blob/main/Sources/ChordCore/Schema.swift for the canonical Swift mappers.

schema.description: Schema identifier. A v3 bump means breaking changes; v2.x adds fields without removing or repurposing existing ones.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp with fractional seconds. Informational only; not a stable sort key.

source_path.description: Absolute path the parser read the config from. `null` when chord was given an in-memory string (test paths).

action_aliases.description: [action-aliases] table — each key is the alias name (without the leading @), each value is the shell-command body. Schema v3 renamed this from `aliases` for symmetry with `input_aliases`.

input_aliases.description: [input-aliases] table (v2.x forward-compat addition) — each key is a logical modifier-set name, each value is a `+`-joined modifier list (e.g. `"ULTRA_LL": "rctrl + ralt + rshift"`). Referenced from a binding's `input = "…"` via the `$` prefix (e.g. `input = "$ULTRA_LL - c"`) — parallels `@name` for shell-action aliases. Alias names that collide with built-in modifier tokens (cmd/ctrl/shift/…) are rejected at load. Absent or empty when the user did not declare any.

validation.description: Populated by `chord config --validate --json`, absent on `chord config --show --json`. Surfaces validation status structurally so CI consumers can branch without inspecting exit codes alone.

passthrough_unmatched.description: If true (default), unmatched events flow through to the OS unchanged.

exclude_apps.description: Bundle-id glob patterns where chord is globally disabled.

binding.description: `source_line` is absent when the TOML parser couldn't track it. `apps` is absent (== unscoped) or an array of bundle-id glob patterns. `condition`, `hold_while`, `action_on_up` are v2 additions and absent when the user did not declare them.

apps.description: Bundle-id glob patterns. Absent = match every app (the user didn't write `apps`); present = explicit allow/deny list.

hold_while.description: Modifier mask tying a variable's lifecycle to a held-down mod set. When all modifiers in this mask have left the OS-side flag state, chord clears every variable this binding's `set-variable` action wrote. Meaningful only when `action.kind == "set-variable"`. Mutually exclusive with `hold_while_timeout`.

hold_while_timeout.description: Inactivity timeout (milliseconds) tying a variable's lifecycle to 'no relevant input within N ms'. Timer is scheduled when the binding's `set-variable` action fires and reset every time a binding gated on the same variable fires (B-α 'reset-on-use' semantics; matches Vim's `timeoutlen`). Designed for keyboards whose firmware emits modifier chords atomically (ZMK macros, Karabiner complex_modifications) — the modifier flag drops between primary keys so `hold_while` would clear the variable before the next primary arrived. Mutually exclusive with `hold_while`. (Added in chord 0.4.0.)

action_on_up.description: Secondary action that fires on the matching key's release. Same shape as `action`. The primary `action` always fires on key-down; both halves are implicitly consumed (the OS never sees the original down or up).

extra_actions.description: Additional actions that fire on the same key-down, in order, right after `action` (Karabiner `to`-array shape). chord's bounded form: a binding declaring BOTH `action-shell` and `action-keys` runs the shell as `action` (it fires first) and posts the keys here. Absent when the binding has only the single primary action.

modifiers.description: All set modifier bits as canonical tokens — both any-side (`ctrl`) and side-specific (`rctrl`) appear when both bits are set on the binding constraint. Alphabetically sorted.

fn.description: Reported separately because `fn` has no left/right variants.

modifier_sides.description: Per-logical-modifier side requirement. Decoupling logical modifier from physical side keeps consumer code clean (checking `ctrl` does not need to OR over `ctrl|lctrl|rctrl`).

side_requirement.description: absent = must NOT be held. any = either side OK. left/right = strict side. both = both sides held simultaneously. Matches `Modifiers.matches(event:)` semantics.

trigger.description: Discriminated by `kind`. Fields not relevant to the kind (`keycode` for non-key triggers, etc.) are OMITTED by the chord emitter — the schema does not require them.

oneOf.description: Wildcard trigger — fires only inside [[fallbacks]] sections. `name` and `keycode` are absent.

action.description: Discriminated by `kind`. Fields irrelevant to the kind are OMITTED by the chord emitter — the schema requires only the fields actually populated per kind.

raw.description: User-typed value, including a leading `@name` if an alias was used.

alias.description: Alias name (without `@`) when the user wrote `action-shell = "@name"`; absent otherwise.

oneOf.description: v2: state mutation. Sets the named variable in the controller's store. value=0 clears the entry. Bindings with a state-gate (`condition`) consult this store on subsequent events.

raw.description: Original `action-set-var = "…"` user string. Present for the primary action; absent for `action_on_up` (no per-action raw).

condition.description: v2: state-gate predicate. Discriminated by `kind` for future expansion; v2 only emits `"variable"`. The matcher consults the controller's store and skips the binding when the predicate is false. Treats an unset variable as 0 — `equals: 0` is the idiomatic "mode cleared" check.

ok.description: `true` iff the validate command's exit code would be 0. Independent of `strict` — `ok` already accounts for it.

strict.description: Whether `--strict` was passed. Lets consumers distinguish 'lenient pass (drops allowed)' from 'strict pass (no drops, no warnings)'.

dropped.description: `name` and `source_line` are absent when chord couldn't attribute the warning to a single binding row.

section.description: Literal TOML section header the warning fired in.

kind.description: Stable discriminator drawn from ConfigWarning.Kind. Renaming any value is a v3 schema bump. v2.x additions are forward-compatible: input-alias-non-string / input-alias-shadows-modifier / input-alias-invalid-body / undefined-input-alias (from [input-aliases] + $-prefixed references); condition-parse-error / hold-while-parse-error / action-set-parse-error (from v2 stateful bindings).

message.description: Human-readable warning line. Format is NOT part of the stable contract — branch on `kind`, display `message`.
